phases.title: Foundation: routing, theme, session, shared backend
phases.description:
Set up the app shell, routing, and shared infrastructure used by every page.

Goals:
1. App.tsx with React Router v7 routes for:
   - `/` (redirect to `/login`)
   - `/login` → LoginPage
   - `/signup` → SignUpPage
   - `/verify` → VerifyPage
   - `/dashboard` → DashboardPage (protected)
   - `/charter` → CharterGenerationPage (protected)
   - `/wizard` → CharterGenerationWizardPage (protected)
   - `/governance` → GovernanceReviewPage (protected)
   Add a `<ProtectedRoute>` wrapper that checks `localStorage['forge_session']` (JSON with id/email/ts); if missing, redirect to `/login`.
2. Shared session helpers in `/frontend/utils/session.ts`:
   - `getSession()`, `setSession(s)`, `clearSession()` against `localStorage` key `forge_session`.
   - `useSession()` hook (with reactive subscription) for components.
3. Shared app-level state in `/frontend/utils/appState.ts` (lightweight Zustand-like or React Context) to hold:
   - `currentOrgId` (string|null)
   - `currentUserId` (string|null)
   - `selectedProjectId` (string|null)
   - `createdProjectId`, `createdOrgId`
   - `selectedArtefactCode` (string|null)
   Implement as a small Context+reducer or a custom store with `useAppState()` hook. Keep it framework-free so any page can read/write.
4. Shared UI shell in `/frontend/components/AppShell.tsx`:
   - Top bar with FORGE Logic title, current user email (from session), and a Logout button that clears session and redirects to `/login`.
   - Renders `<Outlet />` (or children) for the page body.
   - Use shadcn/ui Button + Tailwind, semantic tokens, dark mode safe.
   The shell is rendered by `<ProtectedRoute>` for authed pages.
5. Backend functions under `/backend/auth/`:
   - `getCurrentOrgId.ts` — runs the SQL from `/context/linked-classic-app/lib/getCurrentOrgId.sql` against `forgeSupabaseShadow2`, parameterised by `user.email`. Returns `{ orgId: string | null }`. Read-only — test-run after writing via `execute_backend_code` with `import fn from './auth/getCurrentOrgId'; return fn({ params: {}, user })`.
   - `getCurrentUserId.ts` — runs `getCurrentUserId.sql` against `forgeSupabaseShadow2`. Returns `{ userId: string | null }`. Test-run after writing.
   These functions use `user.email` from the request context (do not pass email through params).
6. After mounting any protected route, the AppShell (or a layout effect inside ProtectedRoute) should trigger `useGetCurrentOrgId` and `useGetCurrentUserId` once on mount and store the results into `useAppState`. If org_id is null, surface a non-blocking toast/banner saying the user is not assigned to an org (matches classic `getCurrentOrgId` failure event).
7. Read `/frontend/orgTheme.css` to find the available CSS variables, then ensure all new components use semantic tokens (bg-background, text-foreground, bg-primary, etc.) so light/dark mode works automatically.

Deliverables:
- /frontend/App.tsx
- /frontend/utils/session.ts
- /frontend/utils/appState.ts
- /frontend/components/AppShell.tsx
- /frontend/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx
- /backend/auth/getCurrentOrgId.ts
- /backend/auth/getCurrentUserId.ts
- Stub page files in /frontend/pages/ for LoginPage, SignUpPage, VerifyPage, DashboardPage, CharterGenerationPage, CharterGenerationWizardPage, GovernanceReviewPage that render a placeholder. Later phases fill them in.

Resources available in the sandbox: forgeSupabaseShadow2 (PostgreSQL), retoolworkflowwebhook (REST API, generic — only one webhook URL is connected), retoolStorage, smtp, ai/anthropic, ai/openAIProvider, restapi/forgeOpenaiApiKey, restapi/loopsApi, restapi/pdfmonkey.

Reference: source files in /context/linked-classic-app/ — main.rsx lists every page; functions.rsx lists global queries and states.
phases.notes: Auth backend lives at /backend/auth/getCurrentOrgId.ts and /backend/auth/getCurrentUserId.ts (read-only, no params; pulls user.email from session). Hooks auto-generated at /frontend/hooks/backend/auth.ts as useGetCurrentOrgId/useGetCurrentUserId. App state lives in /frontend/utils/appState.ts (call setAppState(patch) imperatively, or useAppState() hook). Session helpers in /frontend/utils/session.ts (localStorage key 'forge_session', shape {id,email,ts}). ProtectedRoute renders AppShell whose Outlet receives the matched child route. Sonner Toaster is mounted globally in App.tsx — use toast.* from 'sonner'.

phases.title: Auth pages: login, signup, verify
phases.description:
Build the three unauthenticated pages.

1. LoginPage (/frontend/pages/LoginPage.tsx):
   - Centered card with FORGE Logic title, subtitle 'Sign in to continue', email input, password input (with show/hide toggle via shadcn Button or Input variant), Sign in button, error text area, Forgot password link (disabled).
   - Sign in button calls a backend function `/backend/auth/checkCredentials.ts` that runs `/context/linked-classic-app/lib/checkCredentials.sql` (SELECT id, email, is_active FROM forge_users WHERE lower(email)=lower($1) AND password_hash=crypt($2, password_hash) AND is_active=true). Params: `{ email: string, password: string }`. Returns `{ id: string, email: string } | null` (null on no match).
   - Validate email format + non-empty inputs client-side before calling. On success, write session via `setSession({ id, email, ts: Date.now() })` and navigate to `/dashboard`. On failure, display 'Incorrect email or password.' in the error text.
   - Link to /signup for users without an account.
   - Use shadcn/ui Card, Input, Button, Label.
   - Test-run getCheckCredentials via execute_backend_code with a plausible (likely-failing) credential pair, just to confirm shape.

2. SignUpPage (/frontend/pages/SignUpPage.tsx):
   - Centered card with title 'FORGE Logic™ Sign-up'.
   - Inputs: full name, email, password, organisation name. Icons via lucide-react (User, Mail, KeyRound, Building2).
   - 'Create account' button that validates (all fields required, valid email, password ≥ a configurable min length — use 8 as default) and POSTs to a backend function `/backend/auth/signup.ts`.
   - `/backend/auth/signup.ts` calls the signup workflow via `retoolworkflowwebhook.rawRequest({ path: '', method: 'POST', body: { full_name, email, password, org_name } })`. NOTE: the existing webhook URL is for a different workflow ID than the classic signup workflow (5950224b-47ce-412d-9241-d1d6e436fcb1). Implement the function so it sends the body unchanged and returns the response body verbatim. If the workflow returns a non-`ok` status (`email_exists`, `org_not_found`, `org_ambiguous`, or other), the function should still return it so the UI can map it to a toast.
   - On the UI, map response.status to toasts matching `/context/linked-classic-app/lib/handleSignupResult.js` (ok → success + clear fields; email_exists, org_not_found, org_ambiguous → warning; else → error). Use shadcn/ui useToast / sonner if available; otherwise build a small toast helper.
   - Do NOT call execute_backend_code on signup — it is mutative.

3. VerifyPage (/frontend/pages/VerifyPage.tsx):
   - Read `?token=...` from the URL via `useSearchParams`.
   - On mount, call `/backend/auth/verifyAccount.ts` which runs `/context/linked-classic-app/lib/verifyAccount.sql` (`SELECT status FROM forge_verify_account($1)`) parameterised by the token.
   - Display a centered card with the FORGE Logic logo and a status message:
     - ok → '✅ Your email is verified. You can now sign in.' + 'Go to sign in' button → /login
     - invalid → '⚠️ This verification link is invalid…'
     - already_used → 'ℹ️ This link has already been used…'
     - expired → '⚠️ This verification link has expired…'
     - while loading → 'Verifying your account…'
   - This function is mutative (it calls forge_verify_account). Implement it but do NOT test-run it.

Deliverables:
- /frontend/pages/LoginPage.tsx, SignUpPage.tsx, VerifyPage.tsx
- /backend/auth/checkCredentials.ts (read-only — test-run)
- /backend/auth/signup.ts (mutative — do not test-run)
- /backend/auth/verifyAccount.ts (mutative — do not test-run)
- Toast helper in /frontend/components/ui/toaster.tsx if not already provided by shadcn.

Reference files (under /context/linked-classic-app/):
- src/loginPage.rsx, src/loginCardContainer.rsx, lib/authenticateUser.js, lib/checkCredentials.sql
- src/signUpPage.rsx, src/containerSignUp.rsx, lib/validateSignup.js, lib/handleSignupResult.js, lib/callSignupWorkflow.json
- src/verifyPage.rsx, lib/verifyAccount.sql
phases.notes: Auth backend functions at /backend/auth/checkCredentials.ts (read-only, returns {id,email}|null), /backend/auth/signup.ts (mutative; forwards to retoolworkflowwebhook.rawRequest and returns response.data verbatim with status field; falls back to {status:'error'} on exception), /backend/auth/verifyAccount.ts (mutative; returns {status}). Pages call them via useBackendFunction directly (not the auto-generated auth.ts hooks). Toasts use 'sonner' (Toaster already mounted in App.tsx). LoginPage writes session via setSession then navigate('/dashboard'). MIN_PW_LENGTH=8 hardcoded in SignUpPage.

phases.title: Dashboard page: KPIs and four tables
phases.description:
Implement /frontend/pages/DashboardPage.tsx, matching `/context/linked-classic-app/src/dashboardPage.rsx`.

Layout (wrapped in AppShell):
- Header row: dashboard subtitle '#### Project intelligence, governance status, and delivery readiness.' on the left, and buttons on the right: 'New Project' (→ /wizard), 'Generate Artefacts' (requires `selectedProjectId` in app state; if not set, show warning toast and stay; otherwise navigate to /charter), 'Logout' (handled in AppShell — drop it from the dashboard body).
- Six KPI cards in a responsive grid (md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-6). Use shadcn Card + a `<Statistic>`-like component built ad-hoc (number on top, label below). Cards: Active Projects, Projects Requiring Decision, Artefacts Generated, Open Risks, Runs Completed, Items Needing Review. Values come from a single backend function (see below).
- Four data tables, each in a Card with a title:
  - 'Recent Generation Runs' — columns: Project, Run Status, Current Stage, Completed At (datetime), Action.
  - 'Continue Working' — columns: Project, Stage, Status, Last Updated (datetime), Action. Clicking a row sets `selectedProjectId` in app state and navigates to /charter.
  - 'Risks & Assurance' — columns: Project, Risk / Assurance Item, Severity, Owner, Status.
  - 'Decisions Needed' — columns: Project, Decision Needed, Priority, Due Date (datetime).
  Each table has a Filter/Download/Refresh toolbar (TanStack Table column filters + a CSV export helper + a refresh button that re-triggers the backend hook). Use shadcn Table components (Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableHead, TableCell).

Backend functions under /backend/dashboard/ — all read-only, all parameterised by `{ orgId: string }` (so the frontend passes `useAppState().currentOrgId`). Each runs the corresponding SQL from /context/linked-classic-app/lib/ verbatim, swapping `{{ currentOrgIdState.value }}` for a parameterised `$1::uuid`:
- getDashboardKpis.ts — wraps getDashboardKpis.sql. Returns a single row `{ active_projects, projects_requiring_decision, artefacts_generated, open_risks, runs_completed, items_needing_review }` (cast bigints to numbers via Number()).
- getRecentGenerationRuns.ts — wraps getRecentGenerationRuns.sql. Returns an array of `{ project_name, run_status, current_stage, completed_at, action_label, ... }` rows.
- getContinueWorkingProjects.ts — wraps getContinueWorkingProjects.sql.
- getRisksAssuranceItems.ts — wraps getRisksAssuranceItems.sql.
- getDecisionsNeeded.ts — wraps getDecisionsNeeded.sql.

For each backend function:
1. Read the SQL file under /context/linked-classic-app/lib/.
2. Identify the only required parameter is the org_id (frontend passes `currentOrgId`).
3. Use `forgeSupabaseShadow2("<sql>", [orgId])` (or the matching API for the resource — read /backend/resources/sql/_types.d.ts and /backend/resources/sql/postgresql/forgeSupabaseShadow2.d.ts to confirm the signature).
4. Return the result rows in row-oriented shape — many classic queries returned column-oriented data; here, just return what the SQL returns. The frontend will treat it as rows of objects.
5. After writing each function, test-run via execute_backend_code, passing `params: { orgId: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' }` (or null) just to confirm the function runs without errors and the return shape. Return the result verbatim from the test.

Frontend hooks: use auto-generated `useGetDashboardKpis`, etc., from /frontend/hooks/backend/dashboard.ts. Trigger each on mount with `currentOrgId` as the param; show a loading skeleton (shadcn Skeleton) while loading.

Reference: /context/linked-classic-app/src/dashboardPage.rsx (entire), and /context/linked-classic-app/lib/{getDashboardKpis,getRecentGenerationRuns,getContinueWorkingProjects,getRisksAssuranceItems,getDecisionsNeeded}.sql.

Deliverables:
- /frontend/pages/DashboardPage.tsx
- /frontend/pages/ui/KpiCard.tsx and /frontend/pages/ui/DataTable.tsx (generic TanStack Table renderer with filter + download + refresh toolbar)
- /backend/dashboard/*.ts (5 functions, all test-run)
phases.notes: Dashboard backend at /backend/dashboard/{getDashboardKpis,getRecentGenerationRuns,getContinueWorkingProjects,getRisksAssuranceItems,getDecisionsNeeded}.ts — all read-only, all take {orgId: string|null}, all test-run clean against placeholder UUID. Hooks auto-generated at /frontend/hooks/backend/dashboard.ts as useGetDashboardKpis / useGetRecentGenerationRuns / useGetContinueWorkingProjects / useGetRisksAssuranceItems / useGetDecisionsNeeded. Generic DataTable lives at /frontend/pages/ui/DataTable.tsx (TanStack Table + globalFilter + CSV download + refresh); KpiCard at /frontend/pages/ui/KpiCard.tsx. DashboardPage triggers all five queries on currentOrgId change; row-click on Continue Working sets selectedProjectId via setAppState and navigates to /charter. Logout button intentionally omitted from the dashboard body (handled by AppShell header).

phases.title: Charter generation wizard (FORGE) — Step 1 of 3 + scaffold
phases.description:
Begin implementing /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationWizardPage.tsx, which mirrors /context/linked-classic-app/src/FORGECharterGenerationWizard.rsx.

This phase focuses on:
1. Wizard scaffold and routing.
2. All lookup backend functions consumed by the wizard.
3. Step 1 (Initiative Overview).

Wizard scaffold:
- Page wrapped in AppShell.
- Header: FORGE Logic logo + intro text '##### Capture the essential inputs for a new initiative in four structured steps…'
- Stepper (shadcn Tabs or a simple custom stepper) with three steps:
  1. Overview
  2. Context & Systems (Step 2 will be implemented in the next phase)
  3. Review (next phase too)
- Local wizard state in a small React reducer or Zustand-style store at /frontend/pages/wizard/wizardState.ts:
  - All form values from Steps 1–3 (see classic containerInitiativeOverview.rsx and steppedContainerHomepage.rsx for the full list).
  - currentStep (1|2|3).
  - generationStatus ('idle'|'confirming'|'running'|'complete'|'error'), generationStartedAt, generationResult, generationError, currentRunId.
  - prefill presets (Minimal/Typical/Regulated) — port from /context/linked-classic-app/lib/prefillPreset{Minimal,Typical,Regulated}.js.

Step 1 (Initiative Overview) — mirror /context/linked-classic-app/src/containerInitiativeOverview.rsx (read this file first to enumerate every field). Fields include:
- Initiative name (TextInput, required)
- Initiative description (TextArea, required)
- Initiative budget (NumberInput in £/GBP, required)
- Target start date / Target end date (date pickers — end must be ≥ start + 1 month)
- Service industry select (multi-select from getCreateServiceIndustryType)
- Radio groups: Scale, Complexity, Readiness (sources: getCreateScale, getCreatComplexityLevels, getCreateOrgFamiliarity)
- Lifecycle stage select (getCreateLifecycleStage)
- Multi-selects: Business drivers (getCreateBusinessDrivers), Impacted functions (getCreateFunctions)
- Budget confidence select (getBudgetConfidenceOptions)
- Stakeholder group multi-select (getStakeholderGroupOptions)
- Use shadcn/ui Input, Textarea, Select, RadioGroup, Checkbox, Calendar/DatePicker components.
- Add a 'Prefill' menu (DropdownMenu) with Minimal / Typical / Regulated presets that populate the form.

Backend lookup functions under /backend/wizard/lookups/ — all read-only, no params (org-agnostic dictionaries):
For each SQL file, write a function that calls forgeSupabaseShadow2(sql) and returns the rows. Test-run each after writing.
- getCreateBusinessDrivers (lib/getCreateBusinessDrivers.sql)
- getCreateFunctions (lib/getCreateFunctions.sql)
- getCreateScopes (lib/getCreateScopes.sql)
- getCreateScopesInformation
- getCreateScopesOrganisation
- getCreateScopesTechnology
- getCreateScopesProcesses
- getCreateITSystemsTypes
- getCreatComplexityLevels
- getCreateScale
- getCreateConfidenceLevel
- getCreateOrgFamiliarity
- getCreateDeliveryType
- getCreateServiceIndustryType
- getCreateLifecycleStage
- getDeliveryModelGroups
- getDeliveryModelsForGroup (param: groupValue: string)
- getResourceModelOptions
- getKeyRoleOptions
- getBudgetConfidenceOptions
- getStakeholderGroupOptions
- getSystemCategories
- getSystems (param: categoryId?: string — read /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getSystems.sql to confirm)
- getImpactBuckets
- getOsScopePotiOptions, getOsScopeProcessesOptions, getOsScopeOrganisationOptions, getOsScopeInformationOptions, getOsScopeTechnologyOptions, getOsItSystemTypesOptions
- getProjectsRequiredColumns, getProjectsColumnDefaults, getProjectsDomains, deliveryModelSelect

Use a single shared helper /backend/wizard/lookups/_runLookup.ts that takes a SQL string + params and returns rows, to keep each function tiny. Test-run a representative subset (e.g. getCreateBusinessDrivers, getSystemCategories, getDeliveryModelGroups, getSystems with a null/empty categoryId) after writing.

Do NOT implement Step 2/3/Review or any mutative function (createProject, createProjectContext, createProjectSystems, generation workflow) yet — that's the next phase.

Deliverables:
- /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationWizardPage.tsx (scaffold + Step 1)
- /frontend/pages/wizard/wizardState.ts
- /frontend/pages/wizard/Step1Overview.tsx
- /frontend/pages/wizard/presets.ts (Minimal/Typical/Regulated based on classic prefill js files)
- /backend/wizard/lookups/_runLookup.ts
- /backend/wizard/lookups/*.ts (one per SQL file above)

Reference: /context/linked-classic-app/src/FORGECharterGenerationWizard.rsx, containerInitiativeOverview.rsx, steppedContainerHomepage.rsx, and the matching /context/linked-classic-app/lib/*.sql / .js files.
phases.notes:
Wizard state lives at /frontend/pages/wizard/wizardState.ts (module pub/sub store, useWizardState hook, full Step 1–3 field shape already defined). Presets at /frontend/pages/wizard/presets.ts return Partial<WizardState>. Step1Overview.tsx wires up all overview fields incl. budget confidence, stakeholder groups, and shows a 'pick a delivery model first' placeholder for lifecycle until Step 2 sets ws.preferredDeliveryModelSelect. MultiSelect.tsx in /frontend/pages/wizard/ is a popover+checkbox multi-select generic over string|number. Backend lookups all under /backend/wizard/lookups/ using shared _runLookup helper; hooks auto-generated in /frontend/hooks/backend/wizard/lookups.ts. getCreateLifecycleStage takes {deliveryModelId?: string|null} (defaults to zero UUID = empty rows). getDeliveryModelsForGroup takes {groupValue: string|null}. getSystems takes {categoryId?: string|null} but classic ignored the filter; passing null returns all 105 rows. Wizard page mounts Step 1, has stepper + Prefill DropdownMenu + Back/Next nav; Steps 2/3 render a placeholder. Step 2 should consume useGetDeliveryModelGroups -> useGetDeliveryModelsForGroup (chained on groupValue) and then call lifecycle.trigger({deliveryModelId: preferredDeliveryModelSelect}). systemsSelection rows shape already defined in WizardState.</notes>
</invoke>

phases.title: Charter generation wizard — Steps 2 & 3, project creation, generation kick-off
phases.description:
Complete /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationWizardPage.tsx by adding Step 2 (Context & Systems), Step 3 (Review), the confirm modal, mutative backend functions, and the generation workflow trigger.

Step 2: Context & Systems — mirror /context/linked-classic-app/src/steppedContainerHomepage.rsx (the View labelled 'Context & Systems', viewKey 'Step 3' in the source) and containerScopeDimensions.rsx. Read both files first.
Fields include:
- Strategic Objectives (TextInput, required, with banned-pattern validation — port the regex list from containerScopeDimensions.rsx).
- Business Problem (TextInput, required).
- Delivery model group select (data from getDeliveryModelGroups) + Preferred framework/method select (data from getDeliveryModelsForGroup, fired on group change) + optional Custom name input shown when selectedItem.model_code === 'CUSTOM'.
- POTI checkbox groups: Processes, Organisation, Technology, Information (sources: getOsScopeProcessesOptions, getOsScopeOrganisationOptions, getOsScopeTechnologyOptions, getOsScopeInformationOptions). All four required (non-empty selection).
- Systems picker: a grid of system categories (getSystemCategories) with expand/collapse state per category (`expandedCategoriesState`); inside each, a list of systems (getSystems filtered by category). The user adds rows to `systemsSelectionState`, each with { categoryId, systemId|null, customSystemName?, impactBucketCode, note? }. Buckets come from getImpactBuckets. The summary text 'X in scope · Y integration · Z context' and 'N systems selected across M categories' should reflect state changes live.

Step 3: Review — port logic from /context/linked-classic-app/lib/wizardReviewModel.js. Render a read-only summary of all entered fields grouped by section.

Footer buttons (visible on each step):
- Back (steps 2–3)
- Next (steps 1–2) — disabled until the current step's required fields are valid. Use the BLOCKING checks from the classic Text 'text12' element (in FORGECharterGenerationWizard.rsx) as the source of truth for required-field detection.
- Generate (step 3 only) — runs the create + generate flow described below.

Confirm modal — port /context/linked-classic-app/src/confirmGenerationModal.rsx (read it first). Shown after createProject + createProjectContext succeed and before the workflow runs. Use shadcn Dialog.

Generation error container — port /context/linked-classic-app/src/containerGenerationError.rsx. Shown when generationStatus === 'error'.

Mutative backend functions under /backend/wizard/:
- createProject.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/createProject.sql. Param: a typed object holding every form value referenced by the SQL (initiativeName, initiativeDescription, budget, dates, service industry list, readiness, scale, complexity, lifecycle phase id, delivery model id, business drivers array, impacted functions array). Calls forgeSupabaseShadow2 with parameterised placeholders (the source SQL uses inline mustache expressions — port them to $1, $2, … parameters; do not eval JS inside the SQL). The `current_user.email` references must come from `user.email`, not params. Returns `{ projectId, orgId, serviceIndustry, organisationalReadiness, deliveryMaturity, regulatoryIntensity, projectDeliveryModelId }`. MUTATIVE — DO NOT TEST-RUN.
- createProjectContext.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/createProjectContext.sql. Param: `{ projectId: string, orgId: string }` plus the scope/POTI selections. MUTATIVE — DO NOT TEST-RUN.
- createProjectSystems.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/createProjectSystems.sql. Param: `{ projectId, orgId, selections: Array<{ categoryId, systemId|null, customSystemName?, impactBucketCode, note? }> }`. MUTATIVE — DO NOT TEST-RUN.
- runStagedGenerationPipeline.ts — calls retoolworkflowwebhook.rawRequest({ path: '', method: 'POST', body: { org_id, entity_type: 'Project', entity_id: projectId, initiated_by_user_id, initiated_by_email } }). Returns the workflow response body. NOTE: The configured webhook URL targets a different workflow ID (f30220c3-c5f8-40db-b993-5a79f7f32ce5) than the classic staged-generation workflow (b5ecc0c6-1732-4bc9-a7a9-0e0425e38e5f). The function should still be wired the same way; the user can update the resource to point at the correct workflow.
- pollGenerationProgress.ts — runs /context/linked-classic-app/lib/pollGenerationProgress.sql. Param: `{ orgId, projectId, runId? }`. Returns the latest progress row. Read-only — test-run with placeholder ids after writing.
- finaliseGenerationOnComplete logic moves into the frontend in a useEffect that polls every 3s while status === 'running'; when polled status becomes 'Completed' set generationStatus='complete' and navigate to /charter, when 'Failed' set generationStatus='error'. Port /context/linked-classic-app/lib/finaliseGenerationOnComplete.js and tickProgressNow.js semantics into that effect.

Generate flow (in /frontend/pages/wizard/runGenerate.ts):
1. trigger createProject — capture `projectId, orgId`; store in `useAppState` (`createdProjectId`, `createdOrgId`, `selectedProjectId`).
2. trigger createProjectContext (passing projectId, orgId).
3. trigger createProjectSystems (passing systems selection).
4. Set generationStatus='confirming'; open confirm modal.
5. On confirm: set generationStatus='running', generationStartedAt=Date.now(), trigger runStagedGenerationPipeline.
6. Start polling pollGenerationProgress every 3s. Update generationResult/error/currentRunId as classic does.
7. On completion, toast and navigate to /charter (selectedProjectId is already set).
Port the human-readable error wrapping from /context/linked-classic-app/lib/runGenerateFlow.js and runArtefactsGeneration.js.

Deliverables:
- /frontend/pages/wizard/Step2ContextSystems.tsx
- /frontend/pages/wizard/Step3Review.tsx
- /frontend/pages/wizard/ConfirmGenerationModal.tsx
- /frontend/pages/wizard/GenerationErrorPanel.tsx
- /frontend/pages/wizard/runGenerate.ts
- /backend/wizard/createProject.ts (mutative — no test-run)
- /backend/wizard/createProjectContext.ts (mutative — no test-run)
- /backend/wizard/createProjectSystems.ts (mutative — no test-run)
- /backend/wizard/runStagedGenerationPipeline.ts (mutative — no test-run)
- /backend/wizard/pollGenerationProgress.ts (read-only — test-run)

Reference files: /context/linked-classic-app/src/{FORGECharterGenerationWizard.rsx, steppedContainerHomepage.rsx, containerInitiativeOverview.rsx, containerScopeDimensions.rsx, confirmGenerationModal.rsx, containerGenerationError.rsx} and /context/linked-classic-app/lib/{createProject.sql, createProjectContext.sql, createProjectSystems.sql, runStagedGenerationPipeline.json, pollGenerationProgress.sql, runGenerateFlow.js, runArtefactsGeneration.js, finaliseGenerationOnComplete.js, tickProgressNow.js, wizardReviewModel.js, prefillPreset*.js}.
phases.notes:
Wizard flow end-to-end:
- Backend functions under /backend/wizard/: createProject, createProjectContext, createProjectSystems (mutative, not test-run), runStagedGenerationPipeline (mutative), pollGenerationProgress (test-run OK, returned null with placeholder UUIDs).
- createProject SQL resolves selectLifeCycleStage as either UUID or phase_name/phase_code via delivery_model_phases — wizard state can keep storing label strings (presets pass labels too).
- Service-industry text is computed in /frontend/pages/wizard/runGenerate.ts (buildCreateProjectParams) by joining labels resolved from the industry lookup data.
- /frontend/pages/wizard/runGenerate.ts owns the imperative flow: runCreateProjectFlow → createProject → setAppState(createdProjectId/createdOrgId/selectedProjectId) → createProjectContext → createProjectSystems → setWizardState({generationStatus:'confirming'}). runArtefactsGeneration sets generationStatus='running' and starts the webhook.
- Polling lives in CharterGenerationWizardPage useEffect on ws.generationStatus; calls pollGenerationProgress every 3s using getWizardState() (not the closure snapshot) so fresh currentRunId is sent.
- Step1Overview now refetches lifecycle whenever ws.preferredDeliveryModelSelect changes, so going Step1 → Step2 → Step1 populates the lifecycle Select.
- ConfirmGenerationModal switches body content by generationStatus (confirming/running/error/complete) and uses shadcn Dialog. GenerationErrorPanel is its inner panel.
- Note on webhook resource: runStagedGenerationPipeline.ts posts to retoolworkflowwebhook (which currently targets workflow f30220c3-…); the body shape matches the classic staged-generation workflow so the URL can be repointed without code changes.

phases.title: Charter Generation page: artefact viewer, regenerate, PDF
phases.description:
Implement /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationPage.tsx, mirroring /context/linked-classic-app/src/CharterGenerationPage.rsx + aiPortfolioDashboard.rsx + tabbedContainerProjectCharter.rsx + cardProjectArrivalSummary.rsx + drawerLatestRunDetails.rsx + registerTableRenderer.rsx. Read every one of these files first.

Layout (wrapped in AppShell):
- Header strip with:
  - 'New Project / Programme' button → /wizard
  - 'Re-Generate artefacts' button (disabled while regenerating, or if no org/project selected; shows a loading spinner during the call). On click: trigger runStagedGenerationPipeline (reusing /backend/wizard/runStagedGenerationPipeline.ts) for the selected project. After it returns, re-fetch generation runs + current artefacts + viewer states, show a success toast. On error, show an error toast. Port the script from the `regenerateArtefactsButton` click handler in aiPortfolioDashboard.rsx.
  - Project select (populated from getProjectsForSelector). Selecting a project updates `selectedProjectId` in app state and triggers a page refresh of artefacts. Format option label: `${project_name || '[Unnamed project]'} | ${project_code}`.
  - 'Project arrival summary' card (cardProjectArrivalSummary.rsx) showing high-level metadata for the selected project (read the file to enumerate fields).
- Body:
  - Intro text: '###### The tabs below will provide you with summary information. For the full documents, please use the Preview button and/or Download.'
  - Tabs (one per artefact in `orderedArtefacts.data`) — labels from `artefact_name`, values from `artefact_code`. Use shadcn Tabs.
  - Active tab renders the correct artefact pattern:
    - `pdf_viewer` → shows a Preview+Download row using getActiveProjectPdf data (pdfmonkey_preview_url, pdfmonkey_download_url, pdfmonkey_filename, pdfmonkey_status, pdfmonkey_failure_cause). Embed an iframe or use shadcn Card with the buttons + a fallback message when the preview URL is missing.
    - `dynamic_register` → render as a markdown table (port the dynamic_register markdown builder in tabbedContainerProjectCharter.rsx) with shadcn Table.
    - `narrative` → render sections (title + narrative + optional bullets) as markdown blocks.
    - Use `react-markdown` (add it to package.json if not present) for markdown rendering.
  - Artefact header: `## ${artefact_name}` with `*Version V | Generated DD-MM-YY HH:mm | by email*` (port the renderer logic verbatim).
  - Register table renderer — port /context/linked-classic-app/src/registerTableRenderer.rsx into a `<RegisterTable>` component.
  - Drawer for 'Latest run details' — port /context/linked-classic-app/src/drawerLatestRunDetails.rsx using shadcn Sheet.
  - Preview PDF / Download PDF buttons (disabled when no URL) — call window.open on click.
  - Footer disclaimer text about AI imperfection.

Backend functions under /backend/charter/ — all read-only unless noted, all test-run after writing:
- getProjectsForSelector.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getProjectsForSelector.sql. Param: `{ orgId }`. Returns rows with project_id, project_code, project_name.
- getCurrentArtefactsForEntity.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getCurrentArtefactsForEntity.sql. Param: `{ orgId, entityType: 'Project', entityId }`.
- getCurrentObservationsForEntity.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getCurrentObservationsForEntity.sql. Same params.
- getActiveProjectPdf.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getActiveProjectPdf.sql. Param: `{ orgId, projectId, artefactCode }`. Returns PDF metadata rows.
- getGenerationRunsQuery.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getGenerationRunsQuery.sql. Param: `{ orgId, projectId }`.
- getLatestPdfForEntity.ts — wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getLatestPdfForEntity.sql.

Frontend derived/composed data — port these classic JS queries into client-side utilities at /frontend/pages/charter/derivations.ts:
- artefactMap (lib/artefactMap.js) — group artefacts by artefact_code.
- artefactsByType (lib/artefactsByType.js) — group by `pattern`.
- orderedArtefacts (lib/orderedArtefacts.js) — sort artefacts for tab order.
- activeArtefact (lib/activeArtefact.js) — pick the artefact matching `selectedArtefactCode`.
- viewerStatesByType (lib/viewerStatesByType.js) — viewer flags per pattern.
- latestRunForSelectedProject (lib/latestRunForSelectedProject.js) — pick latest run from generation runs.
- refreshCharterGenerationPage (lib/refreshCharterGenerationPage.js) — orchestration helper; reimplement as a React `useRefreshCharter()` hook that calls all the relevant trigger() functions in sequence.

On mount: read `selectedProjectId` from useAppState. If null, show a placeholder 'Select a project to render artefacts'. If present, trigger getCurrentArtefactsForEntity, getActiveProjectPdf, getGenerationRunsQuery, and any others required. When the user switches tabs, update `selectedArtefactCode` and re-trigger getActiveProjectPdf.

Deliverables:
- /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationPage.tsx (composed of subcomponents below)
- /frontend/pages/charter/ProjectSelectorBar.tsx
- /frontend/pages/charter/ProjectArrivalSummary.tsx
- /frontend/pages/charter/ArtefactTabs.tsx
- /frontend/pages/charter/renderers/PdfViewer.tsx, DynamicRegister.tsx, Narrative.tsx, RegisterTable.tsx
- /frontend/pages/charter/LatestRunDrawer.tsx
- /frontend/pages/charter/derivations.ts
- /frontend/pages/charter/useRefreshCharter.ts
- /backend/charter/*.ts (6 functions; test-run each after writing)

Reference files: /context/linked-classic-app/src/{CharterGenerationPage.rsx, aiPortfolioDashboard.rsx, tabbedContainerProjectCharter.rsx, cardProjectArrivalSummary.rsx, drawerLatestRunDetails.rsx, registerTableRenderer.rsx} and /context/linked-classic-app/lib/{getProjectsForSelector,getCurrentArtefactsForEntity,getCurrentObservationsForEntity,getActiveProjectPdf,getGenerationRunsQuery,getLatestPdfForEntity}.sql + /context/linked-classic-app/lib/{artefactMap,artefactsByType,orderedArtefacts,activeArtefact,viewerStatesByType,latestRunForSelectedProject,refreshCharterGenerationPage}.js.
phases.notes: Charter page lives at /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationPage.tsx; subcomponents under /frontend/pages/charter/ (ProjectSelectorBar, ProjectArrivalSummary, ArtefactTabs, LatestRunDrawer, renderers/*, markdown.tsx). Backend funcs under /backend/charter/ (6 read-only functions, all test-run clean). Derivations and refresh hook are pure-functional ports of the classic JS queries. react-markdown could not be added (package installs disabled during conversion) — used a minimal in-tree markdown renderer (markdown.tsx) for ##/###, bullets, **bold**, *italic*, code fences. Regenerate button reuses /backend/wizard/runStagedGenerationPipeline.ts via useRunStagedGenerationPipeline.

phases.title: Governance Review page
phases.description:
Implement /frontend/pages/GovernanceReviewPage.tsx, mirroring /context/linked-classic-app/src/GovernanceReviewPage.rsx + reviewResultContainer.rsx + container10.rsx + container11.rsx + container13.rsx. Read those files first.

Layout (wrapped in AppShell):
1. Top card 'Initial Information' with three shadcn Selects:
   - Methodology / governance lens (default value 'general')
   - Material type
   - AI relevance (default value 'possibly_ai_relevant')
   Option lists come from the JS modules /context/linked-classic-app/lib/{methodologyLensOptions,materialTypeOptions,aiRelevanceOptions}.js — port them as static arrays in /frontend/pages/governance/options.ts.
2. A large TextArea 'Paste governance text' (autoResize, required).
3. A validation message line below the textarea, computed client-side from the rules in /context/linked-classic-app/lib/reviewInputValidation.js (port verbatim into /frontend/pages/governance/validation.ts). It returns `{ canRun: boolean, errors: string[], warnings: string[] }`.
4. Buttons:
   - 'Review governance pack' (disabled while running or when !canRun) — kicks off the run flow described below.
   - 'Clear Data' — clears all form fields + result state (port /context/linked-classic-app/lib/clearGovernanceReviewForm.js).
5. Loading text 'Reviewing governance pack...' visible while the workflow is running.
6. Result panel (reviewResultContainer.rsx) — read the file and port the layout. It typically renders sections from `governanceReviewExportResultState`. Render markdown sections using react-markdown.
7. Action buttons under the result panel: 'Copy Markdown' (port copyGovernanceReviewMarkdown.js — calls navigator.clipboard.writeText with the built markdown) and 'Export / Download Markdown' (port buildGovernanceReviewMarkdownExport.js — assembles a .md string and triggers a Blob download).

State (component-level useState/useReducer):
- methodologyLens, materialType, aiRelevance, governanceText
- validation (recomputed on each change)
- runStatus ('idle'|'running'|'complete'|'error')
- governanceReviewResult (the captured display/export object)
- currentGovernanceReviewId, activeGovernanceReviewIdState

Run flow (port /context/linked-classic-app/lib/runGovernanceReviewFlow.js + the inline 'Review' button handler in GovernanceReviewPage.rsx):
1. Re-run validation; if !canRun, show warning toast and stop.
2. Show info toast 'Governance review started'.
3. Call /backend/governance/prepareGovernanceReviewRun.ts — port /context/linked-classic-app/lib/prepareGovernanceReviewRun.js. This sets up a review row in the DB and returns the review id. MUTATIVE — DO NOT TEST-RUN.
4. Call /backend/governance/runGovernanceReviewWorkflow.ts — POSTs to retoolworkflowwebhook (workflowParams from /context/linked-classic-app/lib/runGovernanceReviewQuery.json). Returns the workflow body unchanged. NOTE: configured webhook URL is for workflow f30220c3-…, not the governance workflow a2edbd42-…; document this in the function. MUTATIVE — DO NOT TEST-RUN.
5. Pull the result via /backend/governance/getGovernanceReviewResult.ts which wraps /context/linked-classic-app/lib/getGovernanceReviewResult.sql. Read-only — test-run after writing.
6. Capture display/result from the workflow output (mirror the capture logic in the classic 'click' handler — try keys: result, display, display_model, data.result, data.display, data.display_model). Set governanceReviewResult.
7. On success, success toast + render result; on failure, error toast + set runStatus='error'.

Deliverables:
- /frontend/pages/GovernanceReviewPage.tsx
- /frontend/pages/governance/options.ts
- /frontend/pages/governance/validation.ts
- /frontend/pages/governance/ReviewResult.tsx
- /frontend/pages/governance/exportMarkdown.ts (build + download + copy helpers)
- /backend/governance/prepareGovernanceReviewRun.ts (mutative — no test-run)
- /backend/governance/runGovernanceReviewWorkflow.ts (mutative — no test-run)
- /backend/governance/getGovernanceReviewResult.ts (read-only — test-run with a dummy reviewId)

Reference files: /context/linked-classic-app/src/{GovernanceReviewPage.rsx, reviewResultContainer.rsx, container10.rsx, container11.rsx, container13.rsx} and /context/linked-classic-app/lib/{materialTypeOptions,aiRelevanceOptions,methodologyLensOptions,reviewInputValidation,prepareGovernanceReviewRun,runGovernanceReviewFlow,getGovernanceReviewResult,clearGovernanceReviewForm,clearGovernanceReviewResultOnly,buildGovernanceReviewMarkdownExport,copyGovernanceReviewMarkdown,runGovernanceReviewQuery}.js/.sql/.json.
phases.notes:
Governance review page wired end-to-end. Notes for follow-up:
- Backend funcs live in /backend/governance/ (prepareGovernanceReviewRun + runGovernanceReviewWorkflow are mutative; getGovernanceReviewResult is read-only and test-runs returning null for unknown reviewId).
- runGovernanceReviewWorkflow POSTs to retoolworkflowwebhook which currently targets workflow f30220c3-… (same as wizard); the governance workflow is a2edbd42-…. URL must be repointed at the resource level.
- prepareGovernanceReviewRun mints a UUID client/server side; no DB row is written (the workflow itself inserts the row).
- The run flow captures display from workflow body keys (result/display/display_model/data.result/...) then falls back to stored row.result_json.
- exportMarkdown.ts owns three responsibilities: normaliseReviewResult() (workflow body -> DisplayModel), buildGovernanceReviewMarkdownExport() + downloadMarkdown() (.md download), buildGovernanceReviewCopyMarkdown() + copyMarkdownToClipboard() (buyer-friendly clipboard copy).
- Used the existing /frontend/pages/charter/markdown.tsx renderer (no react-markdown install — package installs disabled during conversion, matching the charter phase's approach).

phases.title: Review the conversion for gaps and queue fix phases
phases.description:
Review the completed target app against the source classic app under
/context/linked-classic-app/. Identify conversion gaps, but do not fix them inline in
this phase.

Compare the source and target app coverage for:
- pages/routes
- containers and widgets
- queries, trigger behavior, and query parameters
- event handlers
- transformers, scripts, and state variables

For each material gap, append one focused fix phase with
`extend_phased_build_plan`. Each appended phase should be self-contained and small
enough for one context window.

Keep any comparison notes private or in tool arguments only; do not narrate the full
audit to the user. Do not re-queue gaps already covered by existing phases.

When all identified gaps have been queued, call `complete_build_phase`. If no gaps
are found, call `complete_build_phase` without appending phases.
phases.notes: Two gaps queued: (1) missing Business Case Detail wizard step (fields exist in WizardState but were never editable by the user — only via presets); (2) ConfirmGenerationModal complete state missing Preview PDF / Download PDF buttons, and the page auto-navigates on complete so the buttons would never show — must defer navigation until Done is clicked. All other source-app coverage (auth pages, dashboard, charter viewer, governance review) verified equivalent.

phases.title: Add wizard Step 3 — Business Case Detail (editable inputs)
phases.description:
The classic wizard has a dedicated 'Business Case Detail' view (viewKey '5' in /context/linked-classic-app/src/steppedContainerHomepage.rsx — see lines 466-549, plus container10.rsx and container11.rsx). It exposes editable inputs that the React conversion currently never lets the user edit (they only get populated via Prefill presets). These values are sent to createProjectContext, so they must be user-editable.

Add a new wizard step that lets users edit these fields:
- explicitOutOfScopeInput (TextArea, label 'Explicit out of scope', placeholder 'List anything deliberately excluded from this initiative.')
- knownDependenciesInput (TextArea, label 'Known dependencies', placeholder 'List teams, decisions, systems, suppliers, approvals or activities this work depends on.')
- keyAssumptionsInput (TextArea, label 'Key assumptions', placeholder 'List planning assumptions the business case should rely on, but which still need validation.')
- keyConstraintsInput (TextArea, label 'Key constraints', placeholder 'List known limits, deadlines, policies, capacity issues, technology constraints or governance constraints.')
- resourceModelSelect (Select fed by useGetResourceModelOptions; data shape: rows of {value,label})
- keyRolesRequiredMultiselect (MultiSelect fed by useGetKeyRoleOptions; rows of {value,label})
- budgetNotesInput (TextArea, label 'Budget notes', placeholder 'Enter any issues or constraints.')
- qualityAssuranceExpectationsInput (TextArea, label 'Quality / assurance expectations', placeholder 'List required reviews, assurance gates, testing expectations, compliance checks or sign-offs.')

Group them visually as in the classic UI: 'Scope Boundaries' (explicitOutOfScope + knownDependencies), 'Planning Assumptions & Constraints' (keyAssumptions + keyConstraints), 'Delivery & Resource Shape' (resourceModelSelect + keyRolesRequiredMultiselect — note stakeholderGroupsMultiselect already lives in Step 1, do NOT duplicate), 'Financial, Quality & Assurance Inputs' (budgetNotesInput + qualityAssuranceExpectationsInput — budgetConfidenceSelect already lives in Step 1, do NOT duplicate).

All fields are OPTIONAL (no required flag in the classic UI) — do not add blocking validation.

Implementation steps:
1. Create /frontend/pages/wizard/Step3BusinessCaseDetail.tsx that reads/writes these fields on useWizardState() — fields are already defined on WizardState in /frontend/pages/wizard/wizardState.ts so no state-shape changes needed.
2. Wire backend hooks useGetResourceModelOptions and useGetKeyRoleOptions from /frontend/hooks/backend/wizard/lookups (trigger them on mount via useEffect). Use the existing MultiSelect at /frontend/pages/wizard/MultiSelect.tsx for keyRolesRequiredMultiselect.
3. In /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationWizardPage.tsx, expand the wizard from 3 steps to 4 steps. Update the STEPS array to: 1 Overview, 2 Context & Systems, 3 Business Case Detail, 4 Review. Render Step3BusinessCaseDetail when currentStep === 3 and shift the existing Step3Review to currentStep === 4. Update goToStep / nextDisabled / generateDisabled / completed-step checks to handle 4 steps (the new step has no blocking validation — step3Invalid becomes a no-op so Next stays enabled; the final Generate button still calls the existing step1Invalid()/step2Invalid() guards via what is now step4Invalid()). Widen the WizardState.currentStep type in wizardState.ts from `1 | 2 | 3` to `1 | 2 | 3 | 4` and update related casts.
4. Update the stepper sub-component and the 'Step X of 3' helper text to reflect 4 steps.

Do not change the existing Step1Overview, Step2ContextSystems, Step3Review components (Step3Review will simply be rendered at step 4).
phases.notes:
Added /frontend/pages/wizard/Step3BusinessCaseDetail.tsx (Scope Boundaries / Planning Assumptions & Constraints / Delivery & Resource Shape / Financial, Quality & Assurance Inputs sections). Wizard expanded to 4 steps; new step renders at currentStep===3, existing Step3Review shifted to currentStep===4. step3Invalid is a no-op (no required fields); generation guard renamed step4Invalid. Widened WizardState.currentStep to 1|2|3|4 plus casts in goToStep/Math.max/Math.min/Stepper. Did NOT duplicate stakeholderGroupsMultiselect or budgetConfidenceSelect (still live on Step 1).</notes>
</invoke>

phases.title: Add Preview/Download PDF buttons to wizard ConfirmGenerationModal 'complete' state
phases.description:
The classic confirmGenerationModal (/context/linked-classic-app/src/confirmGenerationModal.rsx) renders three buttons in the 'complete' state: 'Preview PDF' (opens generationResult.pdfmonkey_preview_url in a new tab), 'Download PDF' (opens generationResult.pdfmonkey_download_url), and 'Done'. The React port in /frontend/pages/wizard/ConfirmGenerationModal.tsx currently only renders the Done button — Preview and Download are missing.

Fix: extend the modal props and complete-state body in /frontend/pages/wizard/ConfirmGenerationModal.tsx to render Preview PDF and Download PDF buttons in addition to Done, but ONLY when a URL is available. The generation result object is already stored on wizardState.generationResult (an unknown payload from pollGenerationProgress). It may contain `pdfmonkey_preview_url` and `pdfmonkey_download_url` as top-level string fields.

Implementation:
1. Add two optional props to the modal: `previewPdfUrl?: string | null` and `downloadPdfUrl?: string | null`.
2. In the complete-state JSX render an outline 'Preview PDF' button when previewPdfUrl is a non-empty string (uses <a href target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"> styled as Button via shadcn Button asChild, or a regular Button with onClick=window.open). Render a 'Download PDF' button the same way when downloadPdfUrl is a non-empty string. Keep the existing Done button.
3. In /frontend/pages/CharterGenerationWizardPage.tsx pass these new props by safely reading from ws.generationResult — narrow the unknown via a small helper, e.g.
     const gr = ws.generationResult as { pdfmonkey_preview_url?: string | null; pdfmonkey_download_url?: string | null } | null
   then pass `previewPdfUrl={gr?.pdfmonkey_preview_url ?? null}` and `downloadPdfUrl={gr?.pdfmonkey_download_url ?? null}` to ConfirmGenerationModal.
4. Note: the current page auto-navigates to /charter the moment generationStatus becomes 'complete' (see the completedRef effect). That means the 'complete' state is never seen. Update the navigation so it only navigates after the user clicks Done (move the navigate('/charter') call into the existing onClose handler used as the Done click, instead of firing it from the useEffect). Keep the success toast firing once on transition to 'complete'.

Do not change polling, error handling, or any other modal states.
phases.notes: ConfirmGenerationModal now has previewPdfUrl/downloadPdfUrl props rendered as outline Button asChild anchors in the complete state (only when URL non-empty). Navigation to /charter moved from auto-effect into the modal's onClose (Done) handler — only navigates when status was 'complete' at click time. Toast still fires once on transition to 'complete'.
